sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section I, Histaklut Penimit 6

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section I, Histaklut Penimit 6:1
items.html:
It is impossible for the desire to receive to be revealed in any essence except through four phases, and this is the secret of the four letters of HaVaYaH.
Let us expand this matter a little, to understand well the end that is made by Malchut. We shall first explain what the Kabbalists have defined for us, brought in the Zohar and the Tikkunim: that there is no Light, great or small, in the upper worlds or in the lower worlds, that is not ordered under the order of the four-letter name, HaVaYaH.
This accords with the rule brought in Etz Chaim, that there is no Light in the worlds that is not clothed in a vessel. Meaning: I have already explained the distinction between His Essence and the Light that expands from Him — it is only because of the desire to enjoy that is included in His expanding Light, which is a disparity of form from His Essence, in which, God forbid, this desire does not exist. By this, the expanding Light is defined by the name "emanated": by reason of this disparity of form, the Light passes from being Emanator to being emanated. It has also been clarified that the desire to enjoy included in His Light is also the measure of the Light's greatness, and it is called the "place" of the Light — that is, it receives His abundance according to the measure of its desire and craving to receive, no less and no more, as above.
It has also been clarified that this matter of the desire to receive is the entire novelty that was renewed in the creation of the worlds, by way of literal existence from absence: only this form alone is not included, God forbid, in His Essence at all; only now did the Creator bring it forth for the need of creation. This is the secret of "and creates darkness," for this form is the root of darkness — because of the disparity of form in it, it is dimmer than the Light that expands within it and by reason of it.
By this you will understand that in every Light that expands from Him, two discernments are at once made. The first discernment is the essence of the expanding Light, before the form of the desire to enjoy is revealed in it. The second discernment is after the form of the desire to enjoy has been revealed in it, whereupon it thickens and darkens slightly, by reason of acquiring the disparity of form, as above. The first discernment is the secret of the Light, and the second discernment is the secret of the vessel. Therefore, four phases are discerned in every expanding Light, in the matter of the vessel's activation: for the form of the desire to receive, called the vessel of the expanding Light, is not completed at once, but by way of operator and operated. There are two discernments in the operator and two discernments in the operated, called potential and actual in the operator, and potential and operation in the operated — which are four phases.

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section I, Histaklut Penimit 6:2
items.html:
The desire to receive is not fixed in the emanated except through its own awakening to receive by its own force.
The matter is this: since the vessel is the root of darkness, as above, being opposite to the Light, it must necessarily be activated little by little, by degrees, by way of cause and consequence. This is the secret of "the water conceived and gave birth to darkness" (Midrash Rabba, Exodus 22): the darkness is an offspring of the Light itself and is activated by it, in the manner of conception and birth, which is the matter of potential and actual. For the discernment of the desire to receive is necessarily included at once in every expanding Light, as above; yet it does not rise to the name of a disparity of form until this desire is fixed in the Light explicitly. For this, the desire to receive included in the Light from the side of the Emanator is not enough; rather, the emanated itself must reveal the desire to receive in it, in actuality, from its own side. That is, it must draw abundance by its own desire, more than the measure of the Light of the expansion in it from the side of the Emanator. Only after the emanated has been activated by its own force, enlarging the measure of its desire, are the craving and the desire to receive fixed in it — and then the Light can clothe in this vessel permanently.
It is true that the Light of Ein Sof expands, as it were, over the four phases mentioned above as well, up to the measure of the greatness of the desire from the side of the emanated itself, which is the fourth phase — for otherwise it would not have departed at all from His Essence to be fixed with a name of its own, namely Ein Sof. Nevertheless, in all His power, the form did not change at all on account of the desire to receive, and no difference was discerned there between the Light and the place of the Light, which is the desire to enjoy, as above — they are literally one. And this is what is written in Pirkei de-Rabbi Eliezer, that before the world was created, He was One and His Name One. The doubled phrase "He and His Name" is indeed difficult: before the world was created, what has His Name to do here? It should have said only, "before the world was created, He was One." But the intention is upon the Light of Ein Sof before the restriction: although there is there a discernment of place — a discernment of the desire to receive the abundance from His Essence — yet it is without any change or distinction between the Light and the place. "He is One" — this is the Light of Ein Sof; "and His Name One" — this is the desire to enjoy included there without any change whatsoever, God forbid. And understand what our sages hinted, that "His Name" [Shmo] in Gematria is "desire" [Ratzon] — namely, the desire to enjoy.

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section I, Histaklut Penimit 6:3
items.html:
The totality of all the worlds in the thought of creation is called the Light of Ein Sof; and the collective of the receivers there is called Malchut of Ein Sof.
It has already been clarified, in the matter of "the end of the act is in the preliminary thought," that this is the thought of creation, which expanded from His Essence in order to delight His creatures. And it was clarified that with Him, the thought and the Light are one and the same. By this it is understood that the Light of Ein Sof, which expanded from His Essence, includes the whole of reality before us up to the future end of correction — which is the end of the act — for with Him, all the creatures are already completed in all their perfection and all the delight He wished to give them. This reality, complete in all its need, is called the Light of Ein Sof; and the collective of the receivers in it is called Malchut of Ein Sof.
